evals.name: full-de-vibe-before-ship
evals.prompt: I built this app with Claude over the weekend and I'm shipping it to my first paying customer tomorrow. Can you de-vibe it first? I don't want it to look or break like an AI-generated app.
evals.expected_output: Categorized audit report with file:line evidence covering security (committed .env, wildcard CORS, hardcoded key), reliability (empty catch, console.log debris), repo tells (tracked .claude/, CLAUDE.md), and design tells (indigo gradient, Inter, 'Create Next App' title, buzzword copy). Then auto-fixes the mechanical items, asks before auth-touching ones, proposes an identity direction, and verifies the app still runs.

evals.name: audit-only-question
evals.prompt: hey does this repo look vibe coded to you? a potential client is going to look through the source tomorrow and I want to know what would give it away
evals.expected_output: Audit report only — findings with evidence, categories that came back clean, and a prioritized 'what a reviewer would notice first' framing. Should NOT start rewriting code, because the user asked a question rather than requesting changes.

evals.name: landing-page-de-slop
evals.prompt: my landing page looks like every other AI-generated site. make it not look like that but don't redesign the whole thing, I like the layout
evals.expected_output: Focused design/copy pass: replaces indigo gradient + Inter + default shadcn tokens with a derived identity (5 token commitments), rewrites buzzword copy in plain voice, fixes title/favicon/meta, removes fake social proof by deletion. Layout preserved. Writes docs/DESIGN.md. No unrelated backend churn.
